name: Shade Garden Ideas
description: Hosta combinations companion ferns and shade-tolerant perennials for Connecticut zone 6a gardens. Saved from horticultural sites only.

name: Garden Tools and Trugs
description: Felco shears wooden trugs knee pads soil blockers and hand tools worth investing in. Curated from gardening supply and review sites.

name: Reading Nooks
description: Window seat ideas garden chairs outdoor reading spots and quiet corners for book lovers. No interiors brands just good ideas.

name: Hostas Worth Growing
description: Cultivar close-ups and planting notes for exceptional hostas including June Sum and Substance Halcyon and Frances Williams.

name: Native Ferns for New England
description: Ostrich fern Christmas fern cinnamon fern and interrupted fern in New England shade borders. Sourced from university extension sites.

name: Echinacea and Coneflowers
description: Purple orange and white coneflower varieties that thrive in Connecticut. Companion planting with black-eyed Susans and native grasses.

name: Glastonbury Garden Club Inspiration
description: Ideas shared at or useful for Glastonbury Garden Club programs and the fall plant swap. Mostly saved for personal reference.

name: Outdoor Seating for Readers
description: Garden benches Adirondack chairs and pergola ideas suited to a New England backyard. Good for reading outdoors in summer.

name: Soil and Compost Methods
description: Cold compost bins leaf mold bins and soil blocking for seed starting. Tools and techniques for improving Connecticut clay soil.

name: Black-Eyed Susans and Rudbeckia
description: Rudbeckia hirta and Rudbeckia fulgida varieties for Connecticut sunny borders. Self-seeding and drought tolerant once established.

name: Perennial Border Planning
description: Layout and planting diagrams for mixed perennial borders in zone 6a. Diagrams sourced from extension publications and horticultural journals.

name: Hand Trowels and Cultivators
description: Good quality stainless steel trowels transplanting spades and hand cultivators for perennial border work. Tools that last decades.

name: Astilbe and Foam Flower
description: Astilbe and Tiarella cultivars for moist shaded spots in New England gardens. Useful companions for hostas and ferns.

name: Garden Paths and Edging
description: Bark chip stone and grass paths through shade and perennial borders. Practical edging ideas for maintaining clean borders.

name: Native Grasses for New England
description: Little bluestem prairie dropseed and Pennsylvania sedge for low maintenance sunny borders in Connecticut. Good pollinators.

name: Winterthur and Garden Tour Notes
description: Pins from Winterthur and New England public gardens for planting inspiration. Not shopping links just plant identification and design ideas.

name: Climbing Hydrangea and Vine Plants
description: Hydrangea anomala petiolaris and other self-clinging vines for shaded fences and north-facing walls in zone 6 New England gardens.

name: Watering Cans and Hose Guides
description: Good quality copper and galvanised watering cans and hose guides that protect border edges. Functional and well-made garden tools.

name: Deer Resistant Perennials CT
description: Perennials that hold their own against deer pressure in Glastonbury Connecticut. Includes Russian sage catmint and ornamental allium.

name: Seed Saving from Perennials
description: How to collect and store seed from echinacea rudbeckia and native asters. Simple low-tech methods from extension and seed saving guides.
